summary: Keep the T2b1 source-port command journal production-unreachable while proving its Windows directory-publication durability path on a native runner.
rationale: Issue #394 adds only the sidecar SQLite source-port journal kernel and its tests. The sole workflow change is a Windows-only focused test that calls the real writable-file fsync plus CreateFileW and FlushFileBuffers directory path; it is necessary to prove the no-clobber publication contract rather than to wire the journal into runtime, providers, browsers, Workbench, or CLI.
verification: uv run --group dev python -m pytest tests/test_source_port_command_journal.py -q
uv run --group dev ruff check src tests experiments tools
uv run --group dev ty check src tests tools
uv run --group dev python tools/check_agent_safety_gate.py --base origin/main
uv run --group dev python tools/check_ai_bad_smells.py --base origin/main
uv run --group dev python tools/check_pr_governance.py --base origin/main
deletion_targets: Failed-create target-file leaks that make a safe retry report CREATE_PATH_EXISTS
Full-history validation in ordinary command-journal writer transactions
Public or reverse-imported journal engine authority outside the facade
risks: GitHub Windows Server 2025 proves the native directory flush route, not every enterprise filesystem or installer policy.
The journal remains production-unreachable: no runtime, provider, browser, Workbench, or CLI caller is introduced.
The workflow remains Windows-only for this focused assertion; macOS matrix jobs retain their existing native evidence coverage.
